message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,365 reviews; OpenTable ~4,220 reviews (4.7); Tripadvisor 142 (3.9, #88/281)
- Sentiment: mostly positive overall (strong on OpenTable/Yelp), mixed on Tripadvisor
- Longevity: Opened 2018; ~8 years operating; stable single-owner (Onural Group). Chef Onural has operated Coachella Valley restaurants continuously since 2011 (The Venue, Palm Desert).
- Credentials: ["Chef Engin Onural — Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador","Chef Engin Onural — 27th place, World Sushi Cup/Olympics 2017 (US representative)","Best Chefs America index placement (chef-level)","Palm Springs Life '40 Under 40' honoree, 2013 (chef-level)","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation","Routine inclusion in regional 'best sushi' editorial lists (Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life Asian-restaurants list)","Featured at Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival [UNVERIFIED specific years]"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently for: 'best sushi Palm Springs', 'best sushi Coachella Valley', 'date night Palm Springs sushi', 'Japanese whiskey bar Palm Springs', 'uptown Palm Springs dining'. Does NOT dominate broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' queries (the latter belongs to Nobu Indian Wells, Mikado JW Marriott, and El Paseo concepts).
- Site structured data: Own site (sandfishsushiwhiskey.com) presents clean address, phone, email, and hours in HTML text but does NOT carry visible Schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup. Menus are linked out to Toast and Postmates rather than embedded as structured data. Agent-readability is moderate: legible to text-extracting agents but missing semantic restaurant schema that would enable rich answers.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey in Palm Springs:

## Restaurant Overview

Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey has been serving modern sushi delicacies in uptown Palm Springs since 2018. Chef and founder Engin Onural draws in cultural influences from his world travels to create a truly unique dining experience.

## Reviews - Mixed Feedback

The restaurant receives both praise and criticism:

**Positive aspects:**
- The food is consistently good, innovative, and delicious, with popular dishes like the sampler plate, exceptional octopus, Lobster Zucchini Blossoms, and Tuna Tostadas
- Reviewers praise the knowledgeable and friendly staff
- The patio offers a tucked-away setting under party lights

**Criticisms:**
- The restaurant lists itself as "$$" on OpenTable, but this is completely misleading as prices are sky-high—it should be marked $$$$ instead
- Rolls range from $18-$22, which reviewers noted is expensive compared to the quality offered
- Some reviewers found the prices high compared to other sushi spots

## Location & Contact
The restaurant is located at 1556 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262 with phone number (760) 537-1022.

Based on the latest 2026 information, here are the best sushi restaurants in Palm Springs:

## Top Recommendations

**Sandfish Sushi** sets the standard for refined, chef-driven sushi in Palm Springs, helmed by Chef Engin Onural, blending traditional Japanese technique with subtle global influence. Signature highlights include melt-in-your-mouth toro, pristine hamachi with yuzu, and thoughtfully plated nigiri that leans toward tradition rather than being overly sauced. The whiskey list is one of the most impressive in the Coachella Valley, making it ideal for special occasions or a sophisticated date night.

**Ponzu Sushi** offers a welcoming setting and a broad menu that balances classic sushi staples with creative house rolls, especially popular for takeout and relaxed dine-in experiences. Located by the Palm Springs downtown park, Ponzu offers everything from simple nigiri and hand rolls to elaborate house rolls with sweet and savory glazes. Bento boxes at lunch are a standout value and ideal for casual midday dining.

## Other Popular Options

For casual and dependable sushi, Taka Shin and Ponzu are local favorites. **Happy Sushi** lives up to its name with colorful rolls, combo specials, and an upbeat vibe, offering a great option for groups or travelers looking for a satisfying meal before heading out for the night. This casual restaurant is centrally located downtown, in Mercado Plaza.

Top-rated options also include Joyce's Sushi, Otori Japanese Cuisine, Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, and Hamachi Sushi.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Engin Onural:

Engin Onural is among a small group of sushi ambassadors designated by the Japanese government, and he represented America in the World Sushi Cup. In 2017, he placed 27th at the event.

In addition to his sushi ambassador designation, he was named Best Chef America in 2016. In 2013, he earned the Sushi Proficiency Certificate by the All Japan Sushi Association, making him the only Turkish person in the world to carry it.

Chef Engin Onural is the chef and owner of Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey, and he is a two-time World Sushi Olympics attendee.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sandfish in Palm Springs on TripAdvisor:

Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey is rated 3.9 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #106 of 309 restaurants in Palm Springs. However, it's worth noting that the rankings appear to vary slightly across different sources and time periods, with results showing rankings ranging from #105 to #118 depending on when the data was captured.

Regarding the Travelers' Choice award, Tripadvisor gives a Travelers' Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor. While the restaurant itself appears to be highly regarded by many visitors, the search results indicate it is ranked outside the very top positions among Palm Springs restaurants, though customer reviews are mixed regarding the overall experience.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about fine dining in Palm Desert with focus on El Paseo and Nobu:

## Nobu in the Palm Desert Area

Nobu Indian Wells features the restaurant's signature blend of traditional Japanese dishes with South American ingredients, and Chef Nobu's famous signature dishes include Yellowtail Sashimi with Jalapeño, Rock Shrimp Tempura and Black Cod Miso as well as traditional Japanese favorites and sushi. Reservations are strongly encouraged.

## Best Fine Dining Restaurants on El Paseo

**Seafood:**
- Pacifica Seafood Restaurant offers a variety of fresh seafood dishes, including seafood stew, fish and chips, and Chilean seabass

- Mitch's on El Paseo Prime Seafood offers a variety of prime steaks, sushi, and seafood dishes in a casual fine dining setting

**Steakhouse:**
- Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert provides a sophisticated dining experience and is an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, continuing to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley

**French Cuisine:**
- Le Paon Restaurant is a contemporary award-winning French fine dining restaurant located in the heart of El Paseo

A diverse selection of upscale and casual dining options ensures there's something for every taste on El Paseo.

# Chef Engin Onural's Credentials

**Awards & Recognition:**
- **Palm Springs Life 40 Under 40:** 2013
- **Best Chefs America Index:** Placement confirmed (2016)
- **World Sushi Cup:** Participation confirmed; specific placement and year not detailed in source
- **Sushi Ambassador:** Not explicitly designated in the provided content
- **Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival:** Annual celebrity chef demonstrations mentioned; specific years of appearances listed as 2016 and onward

**Restaurant Opening Years:**
- **The Venue Sushi Bar & Sake Lounge (Palm Desert):** 2011
- **Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey (Palm Springs):** 2018

**Additional Context:**
The source confirms he holds Japanese government sushi certification and is a certified sake sommelier, though these credentials lack specific years. The World Sushi Cup representation is mentioned but without placement details or competition year.

content.text: Search title clearly says "Palm Springs Life's 2014 40 Under 40 Selections" — but the bio and search snippet say 2013. Biennial lists span years; this is ambiguous. I'll flag as moderately uncertain but lean toward "essentially correct (2013/14 cycle)".

confirms: Yelp review volume of 1,365 confirmed by current Yelp listing (1815 photos & 1365 reviews at 1556 N Palm Canyon Dr)
Opened 2018 in uptown Palm Springs; Onural Group single-owner with continuous Coachella Valley operations since The Venue, Palm Desert, opened in 2011
Chef Engin Onural is a Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador / holds All Japan Sushi Association proficiency certification (corroborated by Phoenix Magazine and chef's own site)
Chef Engin Onural placed 27th at the 2017 World Sushi Cup as US representative (corroborated by multiple independent sources)
Best Chefs America index placement at chef level (confirmed via bestchefsamerica.com profile, ~2016)
Surfaces consistently for 'best sushi Palm Springs' and 'best sushi Coachella Valley' — appears as the named top recommendation in editorial roundups; also appears on Visit Palm Springs sushi list

Does NOT dominate 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' — those queries return El Paseo concepts (Mastro's, Le Paon, Mitch's, Pacifica) and Nobu Indian Wells, with Sandfish absent from fine-dining-Palm-Desert results
Own site does NOT contain Schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup — confirmed by inspecting both root and /palm-springs/home; address, phone, email, hours present as plain HTML text
Menus link out to Toast (ordering) and Postmates (delivery); OpenTable handles reservations — not embedded as structured data
Tripadvisor presence at the rating tier claimed (3.9 stars, ranking in the ~#88–106 range of Palm Springs restaurants depending on snapshot — consistent with 'mixed' Tripadvisor sentiment)
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking specifically stated as '#88/281': current Tripadvisor data shows ranking closer to #106 of 309 Palm Springs restaurants — the denominator and rank in the claim are stale/inaccurate, though the 3.9 rating and 'mixed' framing hold

Palm Springs Life '40 Under 40' year: claim says 2013, but the Palm Springs Life URL/index labels the cohort as the '2014 40 Under 40 Selections.' The list is biennial (2013/2014 cycle); the credential is real but the specific year '2013' is at least partially inaccurate per the publisher's own indexing
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable ~4,220 reviews at 4.7 — OpenTable page timed out and could not be independently verified in this session
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation — no independent confirmation found; given the #~106 ranking (outside top 10% of 309 restaurants) this designation is doubtful and should be re-verified
Sentiment characterization 'strong on OpenTable/Yelp' — Yelp confirmed at 1,365 reviews but star rating not independently captured here; multiple reviews flag pricing as a recurring complaint
Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival featured-chef appearances — chef's own site references the festival but specific years remain unconfirmed (already flagged in the claim)
Claim that chef has operated CV restaurants 'continuously since 2011' — The Venue 2011 opening confirmed, but continuity of operation (no gaps) not independently audited
input.notes: Core visibility picture is solid: Sandfish is the named top sushi recommendation in Palm Springs editorial roundups, the chef's major credentials (sushi ambassador, World Sushi Cup 27th, Best Chefs America, PSL 40-under-40) are independently corroborated, and the structured-data assessment is exactly right — no JSON-LD on either the root domain or the Palm Springs location page, just plain-text contact and outbound Toast/Postmates/OpenTable links. Two specific numeric/year claims drift from current public data: the Tripadvisor rank should be ~#106/309 (not #88/281), and Palm Springs Life indexes him under the 2014 cohort (not 2013) although the biennial cycle blurs this. The Travelers' Choice designation is the weakest claim — at his current rank he is outside the top 10% threshold that designation requires, so it should be re-verified before publication. OpenTable review volume could not be confirmed due to a fetch timeout.
